title: An Efficient Framework for Clustered Federated Learning
key_idea: IFCA: iterative clustering of clients into groups with shared models via hard assignment
relevance_to_fedprotrack: competing - primary baseline; uses hard clustering with no temporal prior or concept memory; FedProTrack uses soft Gibbs posterior with transition prior and memory bank

authors: Hyeon-Woo Nam, Moon Ye-Bin, Jaeho Cho
title: FedPara: Low-Rank Hadamard Product for Communication-Efficient Federated Learning
key_idea: Re-parameterizes weight matrices using low-rank factors combined via Hadamard product, achieving full-rank capacity with low-rank communication cost (3-10x reduction). pFedPara extension separates global/local parameters.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: complementary - demonstrates that low-rank parameterization can achieve full-rank capacity unlike standard low-rank decomposition; Hadamard product trick could improve FedProTrack adapter capacity without increasing comm cost

title: FLoRA: Federated Fine-Tuning Large Language Models with Heterogeneous Low-Rank Adaptations
key_idea: Noise-free aggregation of heterogeneous LoRA modules via stacking (not averaging) of local A and B matrices. Allows clients to use different LoRA ranks.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: complementary - shows that naive averaging of LoRA matrices introduces aggregation noise; stacking-based aggregation preserves mathematical correctness. Relevant if FedProTrack aggregates adapter params across concepts.

title: LoRA-FAIR: Federated LoRA Fine-Tuning with Aggregation and Initialization Refinement
key_idea: Identifies two key problems: server-side aggregation bias when averaging LoRA matrices, and client-side initialization lag across rounds. Proposes structure-preserving aggregation + optimization-based post-aggregation refinement.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: highly relevant - the initialization lag problem directly maps to FedProTrack's adapter failure: when concepts recur, adapters must be re-initialized from memory bank models, and naive re-init causes convergence lag. LoRA-FAIR's refinement step could inspire warm-start strategies.

title: Dual-Personalizing Adapter for Federated Foundation Models (FedDPA)
key_idea: Uses dual adapters (global + local) with instance-wise dynamic weighting at test time. Global adapter captures shared knowledge; local adapter captures client-specific distribution. Dynamic weighting handles test-time distribution shifts.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: complementary - the dual adapter idea maps to FedProTrack's shared/private decomposition in the feature-adapter model. Instance-wise weighting is analogous to our Gibbs posterior weighting across concept models.

title: Improving LoRA in Privacy-preserving Federated Learning (FFA-LoRA)
key_idea: Freeze the randomly-initialized A matrix and only fine-tune the zero-initialized B matrix. Halves communication cost vs full LoRA while maintaining performance. Motivated by observation that A learns general knowledge, B learns task-specific knowledge.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: highly relevant - freezing the random projection (A) and only training the zero-init output (B) is a form of identity-init that ensures the adapter starts as identity transform. This is exactly the pattern FedProTrack's feature_adapter should follow for fast convergence under drift.

title: The Impact of Initialization on LoRA Finetuning Dynamics
key_idea: Theoretical analysis showing LoRA converges considerably slower than full fine-tuning. Initialization scheme (which matrix is zero vs random) significantly affects convergence trajectory. Random-A/zero-B yields better performance than zero-A/random-B.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: critical - directly explains why FedProTrack's adapter fails on CIFAR-100: if adapter is not identity-initialized or uses poor init scheme, convergence is too slow relative to concept drift rate. Must ensure zero-init on output side.

title: LoRA-GA: Low-Rank Adaptation with Gradient Approximation
key_idea: Initializes LoRA adapter weights via SVD on estimated gradients to align with full-finetuning direction. Achieves 2-4x convergence speedup over vanilla LoRA.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: complementary - gradient-aligned initialization could help FedProTrack adapters converge faster when switching concepts, but requires gradient computation which may conflict with communication budget constraints.

authors: Panchal, Choudhary, Mitra, Mukherjee, Sarkhel, Mitra, Guan
title: Flash: Concept Drift Adaptation in Federated Learning
key_idea: Detects concept drift via magnitude of parameter updates needed to fit global model to local data. Uses client-side early-stopping (epochs depend on local gradient variance) + server-side drift-aware adaptive optimizer.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: competing - Flash's early-stopping mechanism is directly relevant: it adaptively sets local epochs per client based on heterogeneity, preventing overfitting. FedProTrack's fixed local epochs may cause adapter to overfit or underfit depending on drift speed.

title: FedSA-LoRA: Selective Aggregation for Low-Rank Adaptation in Federated Learning
key_idea: Asymmetry analysis: A matrices learn general knowledge (shared), B matrices learn client-specific knowledge (local). Only A matrices are aggregated server-side; B matrices stay local. Extends to rsLoRA and VeRA variants.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: highly relevant - the A/B asymmetry insight maps directly to FedProTrack's shared/private adapter decomposition. Sharing only the general-knowledge component while keeping task-specific params local mirrors our concept-specific adapter design.

title: Where to Begin? On the Impact of Pre-Training and Initialization in Federated Learning
key_idea: Pretrained initialization provides warm start that reduces training time up to 40%, reduces effect of data/system heterogeneity, and improves final accuracy. The benefit scales with pretraining quality.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: complementary - validates that warm-starting from pretrained features (as FedProTrack does with frozen backbone + adapter) is beneficial, but the adapter itself must also be well-initialized to preserve these benefits under drift.

title: PerAda: Parameter-Efficient Federated Learning Personalization with Generalization Guarantees
key_idea: Each client has pretrained model + personalized adapter + local adapter with skip connections. Adapters initialized to near-zero (identity function). Skip connections ensure pretrained behavior is preserved at init. Provides generalization bounds.
relevance_to_fedprotrack: highly relevant - PerAda's identity-init adapters with skip connections is the standard approach FedProTrack should follow. The skip connection ensures adapter starts as identity, so pretrained backbone features pass through unchanged until adapter learns useful transforms.
